generatedSentence: Leo enjoyed a magnificent evening, seeking relaxation by the lake. He poured himself liquor from a decanter, contemplating his vast estate. An ominous shadow instantly fell over the pines, disturbing his peace. Leo, a retired detective, felt bewitched; this was no ordinary overshoot. A behemoth figure, akin to a rampaging Bigfoot, emerged from the pines. Leo, with vehement bravery, grabbed a heavy container. The creature offered a freebee: a giant watermelon, not violence. Leo realized his abnegation was unnecessary; friendship could be flexible, even with a Bigfoot.
panelSentences: Leo enjoyed a magnificent evening, seeking relaxation by the lake. He poured himself liquor from a decanter, contemplating his vast estate.

imagePrompt: 4-panel comic, 2x2 grid, cel-shaded, adult protagonist, Panel1:[Leo relaxing by a lake on his estate, pouring liquor from a decanter], Panel2:[Leo, a detective, notices an ominous shadow among the pines, looking concerned], Panel3:[Leo bravely confronts a large, rampaging Bigfoot-like behemoth, holding a container], Panel4:[Leo and Bigfoot shaking hands, Bigfoot offering a large watermelon, Leo looking surprised but accepting]

definitions.definition: A person whose profession is cutting (usually male) customers' hair and beards.

definitions.definition: To fit (a baby's bottle, etc.) with a nipple.
To give one's nipple to (a baby) to allow breastfeeding.
A person living on adjacent or nearby land; a person situated adjacently or nearby; anything (of the same type of thing as the subject) in an adjacent or nearby position.
definitions.example: My neighbour has two noisy cats.

definitions.definition: One who is near in sympathy or confidence.
A fellow human being.
To be adjacent to
definitions.example: Though France neighbours Germany, its culture is significantly different.

definitions.definition: (followed by "on"; figurative) To be similar to, to be almost the same as.
definitions.example: That sort of talk is neighbouring on treason.

definitions.definition: To associate intimately with; to be close to.
A closed container for liquids or gases.
An open container or pool for storing water or other liquids.
A pond, pool, or small lake, natural or artificial.
The fuel reservoir of a vehicle.
The amount held by a container; a tankful.
definitions.example: I burned three tanks of gas on the drive to New York.

definitions.definition: An armoured fighting vehicle, armed with a gun, and moving on caterpillar tracks.
(Australian and Indian English) A reservoir or dam.
A large metal container for holding drinking water for animals, usually placed near a wind-driven water pump, in an animal pen or field.
By extension a small pond for the same purpose.
A very muscular and physically imposing person. Somebody who is built like a tank.
A unit or character designed primarily around damage absorption and holding the attention of the enemy (as opposed to dealing damage, healing, or other tasks).
A prison cell, or prison generally.
A metaphorical place where a player goes to contemplate a decision; see in the tank.
To fail or fall (often used in describing the economy or the stock market); to degenerate or decline rapidly; to plummet.
To attract the attacks of an enemy target in cooperative team-based combat, so that one's teammates can defeat the enemy in question more efficiently.
To put (fuel, etc.) into a tank.
To deliberately lose a sports match with the intent of gaining a perceived future competitive advantage.
To resist damage; to be attacked without being hurt.
To contemplate a decision for a long time; to go in the tank.
A small Indian dry measure, averaging 240 grains in weight.
A Bombay weight of 72 grains, for pearls.
To stand; to tolerate.
The collective property and liabilities of someone, especially a deceased person.
State; condition.
Status, rank.
The condition of one's fortunes; prosperity, possessions.
A "person of estate"; a nobleman or noblewoman.
A major social class or order of persons regarded collectively as part of the body politic of the country and formerly possessing distinct political rights (Estates of the realm).
The nature and extent of a person's interest in, or ownership of, land.
An (especially extensive) area of land, under a single ownership.
The landed property owned or controlled by a government or a department of government.
(sometimes derogatory) A housing estate.

A station wagon; a car with a tailgate (or liftgate) and storage space to the rear of the seating which is coterminous with the passenger compartment (and often extensible into that compartment via folding or removable seating).
The state; the general body politic; the common-wealth; the general interest; state affairs.
To give an estate to.
To bestow upon.
(jewelry) Previously owned; secondhand.
definitions.example: an estate diamond; estate jewelry

definitions.definition: Sufficiently.
definitions.example: Are you man enough to fight me?

definitions.definition: Fully; quite; used to express slight augmentation of the positive degree, and sometimes equivalent to very.
definitions.example: He is ready enough to accept the offer.

definitions.definition: A sufficient or adequate number, amount, etc.
definitions.example: Enough of you are here to begin the class.

definitions.definition: Stop! Don't do that any more!
definitions.example: I'm sick of you complaining! Enough!

definitions.definition: Any of several animals related to Equus ferus caballus.
Equipment with legs.
Type of equipment.

A mass of earthy matter, or rock of the same character as the wall rock, occurring in the course of a vein, as of coal or ore; hence, to take horse (said of a vein) is to divide into branches for a distance.
The sedative, antidepressant, and anxiolytic drug morphine, chiefly when used illicitly.

An informal variant of basketball in which players match shots made by their opponent(s), each miss adding a letter to the word "horse", with 5 misses spelling the whole word and eliminating a player, until only the winner is left. Also HORSE, H-O-R-S-E or H.O.R.S.E. (see Variations of basketball#H-O-R-S-E).
(among students) A translation or other illegitimate aid in study or examination.
(among students) horseplay; tomfoolery
To frolic, to act mischievously. (Usually followed by "around".)
To provide with a horse; supply horses for.
To get on horseback.
To sit astride of; to bestride.
(of a male horse) To copulate with (a mare).
To take or carry on the back.
To place (someone) on the back of another person, or on a wooden horse, etc., to be flogged; (hence) to flog.
To urge at work tyrannically.
To charge for work before it is finished.
(of a gun, cannon, Bunsen burner, etc.) To fire in the opposite direction, for example due to an obstruction in the barrel.
(of an engine) To experience a premature ignition of fuel or an ignition of exhaust gases, making a popping sound.
To fail in a manner that brings down further misfortune.
definitions.example: His attempt to make money by importing luxury cars backfired horribly when fuel prices tripled.

definitions.definition: A species of mallow, Althaea officinalis, that grows in marshy terrain.

A type of confectionery, originally (since Ancient Egyptian times) made from this plant, but now generally made of sugar or corn syrup, gelatin that has been pre-softened in water, gum arabic, flavorings, and sometimes beaten egg whites, all whipped to a spongy consistency.
Someone who is soft and benign.
definitions.example: He acts like a total badass, but he's really just a marshmallow once you get to know him.

definitions.definition: To soften (literally or figuratively)
A comic originating in Japan.
An artistic style heavily used in, and associated with, Japanese comics, and that has also been adopted by a comparatively low number of comics from other countries.
A comic in manga style, regardless of the country of origin.
definitions.example: Lately I've been reading a Brazilian manga.

definitions.definition: To move about wildly or violently.
The act of one who rampages.
The act of relaxing or the state of being relaxed; the opposite of stress or tension; the aim of recreation and leisure activities.
A diminution of tone, tension, or firmness; specifically in pathology: a looseness; a diminution of the natural and healthy tone of parts.
definitions.example: relaxation of the soft palate

definitions.definition: Remission or abatement of rigor.
Remission of attention or application.
definitions.example: relaxation of efforts

definitions.definition: Unbending; recreation; a state or occupation intended to give mental or bodily relief after effort.
The transition of an atom or molecule from a higher energy level to a lower one.
The release following musical tension.
(horror fiction) A creature that is undead; that is, dead but still animate.
(horror fiction) Those creatures which are undead; that is, dead yet still animate.
definitions.example: In the zombie movie, an army of the undead accosted some nubile skinny-dipping teenagers.

definitions.definition: Pertaining to a corpse, though having qualities of life.
(horror fiction) Being animate, though non-living.
To shout; to call out.
To express great approval (for).
definitions.example: a highly-acclaimed novel

definitions.definition: To salute or praise with great approval; to compliment; to applaud; to welcome enthusiastically.
To claim.
To declare by acclamations.
To elect to an office by having no opposition.

A hybrid herb of the mint family (Mentha × piperita), formed by crossing watermint and spearmint, which has a high menthol content and a sharp flavor and is used in cooking, especially in herb teas and in confections.
A confection containing extract of peppermint.
A tower or other structure exhibiting a light or lights to warn or guide sailors.
A big cat, Panthera leo, native to Africa, India and formerly much of Europe.
definitions.example: Tigers and lions share a common ancestor from a few million years ago.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Any of various extant and extinct big cats, especially the mountain lion.
A Chinese foo dog.
An individual who shows strength and courage, attributes associated with the lion.
A famous person regarded with interest and curiosity.
A light brown color that resembles the fur of a lion.
An old Scottish coin, with a lion on the obverse, worth 74 shillings.
Of the light brown color that resembles the fur of a lion.
The bags and other containers that hold a traveller's belongings.
The contents of such containers.
A specific bag or container holding a traveller's belongings.
A garden or an area of land for the cultivation of fruit or nut trees.
The trees themselves cultivated in such an area.
First-rate; of the highest quality; exceptionally good.
definitions.example: This champagne is superb.

definitions.definition: Grand; magnificent; august; stately.
definitions.example: a superb edifice;  a superb colonnade

definitions.definition: Haughty.

A hammer that consists of a large, heavy, broad and flat block of metal (the head) attached to a handle typically 0.5 meter to 1 meter long. The sledgehammer's design is meant to allow it to be swung powerfully, and to distribute force over a wide area upon impact.
To strike with a sledgehammer.

To behave as a jaywalker; to violate pedestrian traffic regulations by crossing a street away from a designated crossing or to walk in the part of the street intended for vehicles rather than on the sidewalk.
The act of touching physically; being in close association.
The establishment of communication (with).
definitions.example: I haven't been in contact with her for years.

definitions.definition: A nodule designed to connect a device with something else.
definitions.example: Touch the contact to ground and read the number again.

definitions.definition: Someone with whom one is in communication.
definitions.example: The salesperson had a whole binder full of contacts for potential clients.

definitions.definition: A contact lens.
A device designed for repetitive connections.
(by ellipsis) Contact juggling.
definitions.example: I bought myself a new contact ball last week

definitions.definition: The plane between two adjacent bodies of dissimilar rock.
To touch; to come into physical contact with.
definitions.example: The side of the car contacted the pedestrian.

definitions.definition: To establish communication with something or someone
definitions.example: I am trying to contact my sister.

definitions.definition: A piece of paper that entitles the holder to a discount, or that can be exchanged for goods and services.
A receipt.
One who or that which vouches.
A copy of a published advertisement sent by the agency to the client as proof of publication.
A mechanical device used in shops for automatically registering the amount of money drawn.
To establish the authenticity of; to vouch for.
To provide a vouch for (an expenditure).
To provide (a beneficiary) with a voucher.
One who causes trouble, especially one who does so deliberately.
A complainer.
One who erases.
A thing used to erase or remove something written or drawn by a pen or a pencil.
definitions.example: I have worn out the eraser on this pencil.

definitions.definition: A thing used to erase something written by chalk on a chalkboard, by marker on a whiteboard, or by some other erasable implement; a chalkboard eraser, whiteboard eraser, etc.
An overwriter program used to prevent data recovery.

A digital graphic icon with a unique code point used to represent a concept or object, originally used in Japanese text messaging but since adopted internationally in other contexts such as social media.
A strip of fabric worn around the head.
A hair-accessory, made of a flexible material and curved like a horseshoe, for holding one's hair back.
A strip of fabric attached to the top of the spine of a book; used as decoration and reinforcement.
A thin slip of iron on the tympan of a printing-press.
At once; without delay.
definitions.example: When the neighbours' dog barked, ours instantly replied with a howl.

definitions.definition: Urgently; with insistence.
At the same time.
A person in desperate circumstances or who is at the point of desperation, such as a down-and-outer, addict, etc.
In dire need of something.
definitions.example: I hadn't eaten in two days and was desperate for food.

definitions.definition: Being filled with, or in a state of despair; hopeless.
definitions.example: I was so desperate at one point, I even went to see a loan shark.

definitions.definition: Without regard to danger or safety; reckless; furious.
definitions.example: a desperate effort

definitions.definition: Beyond hope; causing despair; extremely perilous; irretrievable.
definitions.example: a desperate disease;  desperate fortune

definitions.definition: Extreme, in a bad sense; outrageous.
Extremely intense.
To make (someone) a deputy; to officially empower.
To make or name as a substitute.
definitions.example: I deputize you to act for me while I'm away.

definitions.definition: To act as a deputy.
A false belief that is resistant to confrontation with actual facts.
The state of being deluded or misled, or process of deluding somebody.
definitions.example: It is far better to grasp the universe as it really is than to persist in delusion, however satisfying and reassuring.

definitions.definition: That which is falsely or delusively believed or propagated; false belief; error in belief.

A portal of entry into a building, room, or vehicle, consisting of a rigid plane movable on a hinge. Doors are frequently made of wood or metal. May have a handle to help open and close, a latch to hold the door closed and a lock that ensures the door cannot be opened without the key.
definitions.example: I knocked on the vice president's door

definitions.definition: Any flap, etc. that opens like a door.
definitions.example: the 24 doors in an Advent calendar

definitions.definition: (immigration) An entry point.
A means of approach or access.
definitions.example: Learning is the door to wisdom.

definitions.definition: A barrier.
definitions.example: Keep a door on your anger.

definitions.definition: A software mechanism by which a user can interact with a program running remotely on a bulletin board system. See BBS door.
To cause a collision by opening the door of a vehicle in front of an oncoming cyclist or pedestrian.
(usually uncountable) Being brave, courageousness.
A brave act.
Splendor, magnificence
A small square cardboard mat, often with an advertisement for a brewery on it, that is used to rest one's glass on, and to protect the surface of a table or a bar in a pub.
A tree of the Acer genus, characterised by its usually palmate leaves and winged seeds.
The wood of such a tree, prized for its hardness and attractive appearance
